turns.name: JOHN ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the Court in two cases this morning.

turns.name: SAMUEL ALITO
turns.text: The first is Abitron v. Hetronic. Sections 32 and 43 of the Lanham Act prohibit the unauthorized use in commerce of protected trademarks when the use is likely to cause confusion. After a trial, a company called Hetronic International, received a large damages award on claims that five foreign entities infringed its trademark rights both domestically and globally. We granted certiorari to review the Tenth Circuit's affirmance and based on the presumption against extraterritoriality we now vacate the judgment and remand the case for further proceedings. Justice Jackson has filed the concurring opinion. Justice Sotomayor has filed an opinion concurring in the judgment, which is joined by the Chief Justice, Justice Kagan and Justice Barrett.
